skill.name: makepad-skills
skill.description: Makepad UI development skills for Rust apps: setup, patterns, shaders, packaging, and troubleshooting.
skill.summary: Guides Makepad UI setup, patterns, shaders, packaging, and troubleshooting for Rust applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two hardcoded URLs and two weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. The URLs are documentation links to a GitHub source repository, and the cryptography matches are textual false positives with no cryptographic operation present. No prompt injection, command execution, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22 point to the skill source repository. They are static documentation references, not executable network requests or data transmission paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations contain GitHub repository references in Markdown or frontmatter. No code path performs a network request or sends local data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is a Text Match Only
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20 occur in prose describing Makepad UI development. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption code, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive text and metadata. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.

content.value_statement: Makepad projects can be hard to start, structure, and package without focused guidance. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Makepad UI support for Rust applications.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Makepad for Rust UI development.
Guides project setup for Makepad-based Rust applications.
Suggests UI patterns for Makepad screens and components.
Helps reason about shader usage in Makepad interfaces.
Supports packaging and troubleshooting discussions for Makepad apps.
limitations: It contains high-level guidance, not a full Makepad reference manual.
It does not include executable scripts or automated project generators.
It relies on the assistant and local project files for implementation details.
It does not verify platform packaging results by itself.

use_cases.title: Start a Makepad App
use_cases.description: Plan the structure and first screens for a new Rust application using Makepad.

use_cases.title: Improve UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Review Makepad component organization, layout choices, and interface patterns.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Packaging
use_cases.description: Identify likely causes of Makepad build, shader, or packaging problems.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a First Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the makepad-skills guidance to help me plan a simple Makepad Rust app screen. Explain the project structure and first implementation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review a UI Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Makepad UI layout and suggest improvements for component structure, state handling, and visual organization.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Shader Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me diagnose a Makepad shader or rendering issue. Ask for the relevant files, then explain likely causes and targeted fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through packaging a Makepad Rust application for release. Include build checks, platform concerns, and troubleshooting steps.

output_examples.input: I need a starting plan for a Makepad desktop app.
output_examples.output: A concise setup plan with recommended project structure, first screen responsibilities, and next implementation steps.

output_examples.input: My Makepad layout is hard to maintain.
output_examples.output: A review of component boundaries, layout responsibilities, state flow, and practical refactoring suggestions.

output_examples.input: The app builds locally but fails during packaging.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering build environment, assets, shaders, platform targets, and release configuration.

best_practices: Share relevant Makepad files and the target platform before asking for debugging help.
Ask for focused reviews of setup, UI patterns, shaders, or packaging separately.
Validate suggested changes with a local build and platform-specific packaging test.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for complete Makepad documentation from this compact skill.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for running builds or release tests.
Do not mix unrelated UI, shader, and packaging problems in one request.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps with Makepad UI setup, patterns, shaders, packaging, and troubleshooting for Rust applications.

faq.question: Does it create a Makepad project automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance that an assistant can apply while working with your project files.

faq.question: Can Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill declares support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains documentation text and source links, with no executable risky behavior found.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low instead of safe?
faq.answer: Static analysis found false positives that were documented for transparency. No confirmed security issue was found.

faq.question: What should I provide when asking for help?
faq.answer: Provide the relevant Makepad files, your target platform, the observed error, and the result you expect.
